name: archetypes
notes: 4 unimplemented blocking gate ids in vague-to-system validations.yaml re-bound to registry checks (pre-existing v1 bug); tfc-builder MCP host needs restart to serve new dist

exit_gate.result: vague-to-system=eval_proven (behavioral_score 1.0 over 3 golden tasks, observable must/must_not); bump 1.0.0->1.0.1 demotes to authored; lane-gate rejects malformed/below-threshold/<3-task reports (exit 2)

notes: fixed latent install PATH_ESCAPE bug — ~/.future-code is a symlink, so allowed-root check now realpaths both sides

exit_gate.target: one skill recomputes to evolution_proven from a real measured improvement; CHANGELOG>=1; stable across restart
exit_gate.result: vague-to-system: added a failing compound-intent golden task (honest demote 1.0->0.75->authored), evolved SKILL.md compound-intent rule, re-eval 1.0 at v1.1.0, CHANGELOG delta 0.25 consuming 3 real learnings -> evolution_proven, byte-stable across 2 recomputes

name: one currency across the ecosystem

exit_gate.target: a pack requiring min_lane:eval_proven paired to an authored skill is flagged AND doctor reports lanes+drift AND both reachable as MCP tools
exit_gate.result: tfc_pack_bridge flags below_floor (PX1 authored<eval_proven, unit-proven); tfc_doctor aggregates lane per skill + cacheDrift/evalStale/evolvePending + INV-6 stray-state; both registered (restart to serve); dogfood: 5 builder skills graded (vague-to-system=evolution_proven, 4 authored); pack-bridge is read-only (byte-identical packs.yaml)

notes: lane.ts hardened against null/empty spec.yaml (INV-2) — exposed by doctor's full-skill scan; only pre-existing skill-symlinks check fails (2 uninstalled skills), both new checks pass

name: authoring polish — tfc_compile intent front door

exit_gate.target: tfc_compile emits a SkillCard carrying lane: authored + 3 eval seeds; gated behind W1-W4 green
exit_gate.result: node dist/cli.js compile --intent '...' emits SkillCard with literal 'lane: authored' (grep exit 0) + 3 eval_seeds (kebab-task-1..3) + pass_threshold; BAD_INPUT on intent <5 words ('describe the job, not the feature'); archetype inferred from verbs; search-first (tfc_list+spawner_skills+overlap_verdict); voicefix contract threaded in

notes: AS-BUILT DIVERGENCES from W6 GOAL: (1) physical per-archetype _template/ dirs (A#2) NOT built — scaffold already stamps archetype (W1) + compile infers it from verbs; born-loop-ready delivered via the SkillCard's lane+eval_seeds, not template variants. (2) voicefix wired INTO tfc_compile as the drafting voice contract, NOT as a separate tfc_validate --fix mode (A#4 deferred — would muddy validate's Result union). Binding DONE-WHEN gate (tfc_compile) fully met.

name: continuous capture wired live (tfc_capture)

exit_gate.target: at least one skill gains a learnings+runs row from a REAL invocation, and tfc_capture --audit reports the true neverInvoked set; INV-8 holds (capture never writes a learning)
exit_gate.result: tfc_capture (audit/wire) added; install.ts already injected the hook (verified) so wireCapture bulk-injected the 4 unwired skills (idempotent skip of 3); doctor gains capture-wired check + captureWired/neverInvoked fields. DOGFOOD: real learn-itr invocation via the SKILL.md hook path produced learnings.jsonl=1 (via tfc_learn, not seeded) + runs.jsonl row; audit shows learn-itr neverInvoked false; capture-wired passes 7/7 (4 honestly never-invoked).

notes: DOC DIVERGENCE (trust disk, doc 0.4): runs.jsonl lives at REPO-ROOT analytics/ (where runtime/preamble.sh writes via $home/analytics), NOT mcp/tfc-builder/analytics/ as doc 7's re-ground cmd + sharp-edge claimed; capture.ts reads TFC_HOME/analytics to match the hook. wireCapture returns {wired:[...]} array (superset of doc's single {injected,hookPath}) to also serve bulk repair. neverInvoked is honest-empty, NOT a doctor failure (INV-8 / markdown-as-code rule 4). install.ts injection was ALREADY built (doc anticipated 'verify it fires').

name: route repair + reachability precondition (tfc_relink)

exit_gate.target: doctor reports zero broken links; an unreachable skill recomputes effectiveLane:blocked without losing its earned lane
exit_gate.result: tfc_relink added (create missing/dangling, de-dup byte-identical copies, REPORT real conflicts). lane.ts gained a pure reachable INPUT -> effectiveLane:blocked (INV-7 preserved, no Date.now). install.ts: SkillEntry +effectiveLane/+reachable, exported skillReachable; tfcLane wired to it; doctor symlink fix points at tfc relink. PROVEN: video-prompting+genesis repaired missing->reachable; genius-ai (eval_proven) demonstrated effectiveLane:blocked with earned lane preserved BEFORE the incident. PARTIAL on DONE-WHEN: doctor still flags genius-ai broken because its content was lost (see incident), not a mechanism failure.

incident.what: first cut of relink unlink-ed genius-ai/SKILL.md and wrote a self-symlink, destroying 726 lines of content
incident.root_cause: ~/.claude/skills/genius-ai was an OLDER dir-symlink to the source dir; the 'regular file' relink saw at .../SKILL.md was the SOURCE viewed through that symlink (byte-identical because SAME FILE). The de-dup path mutated through the parent symlink.
incident.recovery: UNRECOVERABLE: not git-tracked (dir is ?? untracked), no dangling blobs, no trash, no other copy under /home/roshish. spec.yaml+evals.yaml+eval-report.json+CHANGELOG survived.
incident.fix: relink now inspects the claude PARENT dir; if it is a symlink it is NEVER mutated through (resolves-to-source -> 'ok' left untouched; else -> 'conflict' reported). Re-verified: relink --dry-run on genius-ai now reports claude:ok, 0 mutations.
incident.user_decision: leave genius-ai broken, continue build (rm of self-symlink blocked by permission settings; functionally == missing, NOT_FOUND either way)
incident.followup: genius-ai content needs human restore OR reconstruction from the (complete) surviving spec.yaml+evals.yaml, then re-eval to re-earn eval_proven honestly

notes: Two install conventions exist in the wild: file-symlink (install.ts standard) and dir-symlink (genius-ai legacy). relink is now safe against both; it does NOT auto-normalize legacy dir-symlinks (reports them). checkLinkState still only recognizes the file-symlink convention — acceptable since the recovery path normalizes to convention 1.

name: perishable proof / decay overlay (tfc_decay)

exit_gate.target: a far-future as-of reports stale + a dropped effectiveLane, while recomputeLane stays pure and byte-deterministic (INV-7)
exit_gate.result: tfc_decay (laneAsOf) added: reads eval-report.json.ts (eval_proven) / latest CHANGELOG.jsonl.ts (evolution_proven) vs explicit asOf + spec.yaml freshness_horizon. DOGFOOD: vague-to-system --as-of 2099-01-01 -> stale=true, effectiveLane eval_proven (dropped from evolution_proven, ageDays 26498 > 60); --as-of 2026-06-15 -> fresh, no drop. INV-7 HELD: lane.js free of Date.now (grep -L non-empty), 2 lane runs byte-identical; decay also deterministic for a fixed asOf. asOf defaults to now ONLY at the handler boundary, never the verdict.

tests: 157/157 green (authoring snapshot updated for intentional _template freshness_horizon block)
notes: Semantics: spec WITHOUT freshness_horizon => no decay pressure (markdown-as-code back-compat); WITH the block => per-field default eval30/evo60. authored never perishes. decay is a SEPARATE overlay (does not fold in reachability); W5 portfolio composes both. Gotcha fixed: tsconfig keeps comments, so the literal 'Date.now' in a lane.ts COMMENT tripped the INV-7 grep — reworded to 'never reads the clock'.

name: eval replay / stability quorum (tfc_replay)

exit_gate.target: an aggregate over >=3 samples reports stable, and an artificially high-variance set reports stable:false (proving the quorum bites)
exit_gate.result: tfc_replay (buildReplayPrompt) wraps the single-run eval prompt to run N independent samples -> numbered eval-report.<i>.json, then runtime/replay-aggregate.sh reduces to {n,mean,stdev,min,pass_threshold,stable} where stable = stdev<=0.05 AND min>=threshold. DOGFOOD: stable trio (3x 1.0) -> stable:true; noisy trio (1.0/0.5/0.0) -> stable:false (stdev 0.4082, min 0.0). lane.ts gained an OPTIONAL replay.stable:false hard-veto on promotion (absent => unchanged, back-compat).

notes: Dogfooded the aggregate on /tmp sample files (NOT the skill dir) to respect INV-6 (eval-report.*.json are not contract files -> would be flagged stray) AND because rm is blocked in this session. The replay PROMPT instructs Claude to write numbered samples then DELETE them after promoting -> no stray state in the real flow. Reuses buildEvalPrompt (no logic duplication). Guards VARIANCE only; eval still scores observable must/must_not (eval-theater unchanged).

name: portfolio currency rollup (tfc_portfolio)

exit_gate.target: the rollup reproduces the lane histogram and flags any pack paired below its min_lane, reading only existing contract files
exit_gate.result: tfc_portfolio (rollup) added: histogram {authored:4,eval_proven:1,evolution_proven:1,unknown:1} EXACTLY matches tfc_list lane counts; decayPressure composes core/decay.ts (as-of 2099 -> 2 stale); evolveReady = eval_proven + >=3 unconsumed learnings; belowFloor composes the read-only packbridge; unreachable folds in W2 (genius-ai:1). INV-6 held (reads only contract files + packs.yaml, no new store). INV-1: no API.

notes: Surfaced as a separate tfc_portfolio tool (the doc allowed tfc_doctor --portfolio as an alternative; separate tool is cleaner + matches VERIFY `node dist/cli.js portfolio`). histogram is of EARNED lanes (matches tfc_list); decay/reachability are separate dimensions so the histogram stays comparable. asOf defaults to now at the boundary.

name: authoring polish — born decay-aware + observability-wired (tfc_compile)

exit_gate.target: tfc_compile emits a SkillCard born with a lane, a freshness horizon, and a wired capture hook — so a brand-new skill enters the living loop with zero manual steps
exit_gate.result: SkillCard now carries lane: authored (v2) + freshness_horizon{eval_days:30,evolution_days:60} (W3) + a next-step that tfc_install auto-injects the runtime capture hook (W1). compile.ts STEP 3 renamed SEED THE LIVING LOOP and states the perishability + observability. VERIFY: compile output greps lane:authored ✓, freshness_horizon ✓, capture-hook note ✓; BAD_INPUT on <5 words still bites.

notes: Sequence guard honored (authoring polish LAST). No physical per-archetype _template dirs (carried-forward v2 W6 divergence); born-loop-ready delivered via the SkillCard contract, not template variants. compile.test did not snapshot the full prompt so no snapshot churn.
